run_summary: Screen 1 faces a major basket-rule decision: 9 flags from the Meta WhatsApp agent trigger (5 already held + 4 new), all OVERDONE with confidence 3-4, threat_assessment ranging from 'none' to 'minimal', panic_calibration 'unjustified'. Plus 1 flag (MANH) from the OpenAI Codex trigger also already held. Basket rule applies — all Meta-trigger flags default to exploratory tier; evaluate position exits on day-2 underwater names given thesis integrity remains intact.

entries.name: Blackbaud, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Meta's AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally
entries.thesis: Meta's WhatsApp Business agent targets retail/SMB customer support workflows; Blackbaud's core franchise is donor management, fundraising, and grantmaking for nonprofits with 20+ year donor histories and 3-year subscription terms with ~40% of contracts renewing in 2026. The Meta trigger has no mechanism to displace that workflow, yet BLKB took a sharp drop on no company-specific news.
entries.what_kills: Evidence that nonprofit customers are actually evaluating Meta/WhatsApp-based donor engagement as a replacement, or a Q-over-Q acceleration in churn.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: BASKET RULE APPLIES: 9 flags from Meta WhatsApp trigger (including already-held BLKB). Threat_assessment='none', panic_calibration='unjustified' with crisp filings evidence (nonprofit donor management, 3-year subscriptions, 40% 2026 renewals, zero overlap with retail/SMB customer-service automation). Confidence=4. However, because 8 other names in the same basket clear BUY eligibility, this becomes a single conviction bet on the Meta trigger reading, not nine independent sympathy fades. Default all to exploratory tier per basket rule. BLKB already held in 2 positions; this flag is third entry from same trigger — treat as reconfirmation of existing thesis, not a new position. Exploratory tier allows consolidation of conviction without over-concentration.

red_team.weakest_link: The thesis relies on nonprofit donor-management workflows being structurally immune to AI-agent disruption, but the claim lacks evidence that Meta's agent cannot eventually be adapted or that customers won't experiment with cheaper alternatives.
red_team.critique: The bull case argues that 20-year donor histories and 3-year subscription terms create lock-in, but neither fact prevents churn if nonprofits decide to pilot WhatsApp-based engagement as a supplement or partial replacement. The thesis names 'zero overlap' with retail/SMB customer-service workflows, yet nonprofits do engage donors via messaging and could theoretically use WhatsApp agents for initial donor triage. The 5-day move is -6.42%, suggesting the market is repricing but not panicking; the move is modest enough that a genuine structural threat would likely show sharper repricing.

entries.name: Varonis Systems, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Meta's AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally
entries.thesis: Varonis is structurally a beneficiary of more enterprise AI deployments — data classification and access governance is the substrate AI agents need. WhatsApp Business agent has no mechanism to displace enterprise data security. Today's drop is pure cyber/SaaS sympathy beta to AI-fear flow.
entries.what_kills: Customer disclosure showing AI displacing data-governance spend rather than augmenting it; a slowdown in SaaS conversion.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: BASKET RULE: Meta WhatsApp trigger. Threat_assessment='none', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Filings show AI adoption is demand driver for data-governance spend ('AI-driven applications expand types of sensitive assets...increases demand for data security'). AllTrue.ai acquisition adds concrete AI-security capability. Confidence=4. Basket rule applies — exploratory tier despite 'none' threat_assessment because this is one of 9 names from the same trigger. VRNS already held; this is reconfirmation. Exploratory sizing prevents over-concentration on Meta trigger reading.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case asserts that AI deployments increase data-governance demand, but provides no quantified evidence that enterprise adoption curves are accelerating or that AllTrue.ai's capability is differentiated enough to drive incremental revenue.
red_team.critique: The thesis claims AI-driven applications expand demand for data security, but this is stated as a principle rather than demonstrated by booking trends or customer feedback. AllTrue.ai is cited as a concrete capability add, but the acquisition's revenue contribution or customer-acquisition impact is not specified. Notably, the 5-day move is +10.81%—the stock has already rebounded sharply from the initial sympathy fade, suggesting the market has already repriced the 'overblown threat' narrative. A third entry from the same Meta trigger may be riding existing recovery momentum rather than discovering fresh alpha.

entries.name: OneSpan Inc.
entries.catalyst: Meta's AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally
entries.thesis: OneSpan's authentication franchise is governed by PSD2/eIDAS/FIDO standards and embedded in bank infrastructure. A WhatsApp customer-service agent has zero mechanism to displace e-signature or hardware-token auth in regulated banking. Drop appears to be cyber/auth sympathy fade.
entries.what_kills: Evidence banks are shifting auth workflows into general-purpose AI/messaging stacks (highly unlikely given regulatory regime).
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: BASKET RULE: Meta WhatsApp trigger. Threat_assessment='none', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Core business is regulated bank authentication (FIDO2, Digipass, PSD2/eIDAS standards) — zero overlap with customer-service messaging agents. Filings emphasize banking dependencies, not AI displacement. Confidence=4. Basket rule applies — exploratory tier. Fresh name not yet held; add to basket at 6% exploratory sizing.

red_team.weakest_link: The thesis that banking regulation makes OneSpan's e-signature and authentication workflows immune to disruption is sound, but the bull case does not address whether enterprise IT budgets are shifting away from specialized auth vendors toward horizontal AI platforms, independent of the Meta catalyst.
red_team.critique: The bull case correctly identifies that PSD2/eIDAS/FIDO standards are regulatory moats, but it does not acknowledge that banks' broader digitalization strategies—driven by competitive pressure and AI tooling hype—could lead them to reduce vendor complexity by consolidating auth into broader platforms. The 5-day move is +6.49%, indicating the stock has already recovered sharply from the sympathy fade; the thesis may be correct but the move is already priced. The confidence_in_decision is 4 despite this being a 'fresh name,' which suggests conviction exceeds what the trigger event alone warrants.

entries.name: Paycom Software, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Meta's AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally
entries.thesis: Payroll is a regulated money-movement business (941s, state UI registrations, jurisdictional employment law) — Meta's WhatsApp customer-service agent has zero mechanism to handle this workflow. PAYC has its own real challenges (Beti adoption, sales execution) but they are unrelated to messaging-platform agents.
entries.what_kills: Acceleration of agentic back-office tools taking real PAYC client share — not credible in the next several quarters.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: BASKET RULE: Meta WhatsApp trigger. Threat_assessment='minimal', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Core business is payroll tax compliance, ACH money movement, jurisdictional employment law filings — not addressable by customer-service messaging agents. Filings acknowledge AI as both risk and opportunity but core moat is regulated money-movement, not text generation. Confidence=4. Basket rule applies — exploratory tier. Fresh name, add at 6%.

red_team.weakest_link: The thesis that regulated money-movement and tax-filing are immune to AI disruption is structurally sound, but the bull case does not address whether PAYC's real competitive risks (Beti adoption, sales execution) are being masked by the sympathy-fade narrative, allowing deteriorating fundamentals to hide.
red_team.critique: The bull case explicitly acknowledges 'PAYC has its own real challenges (Beti adoption, sales execution) but they are unrelated to messaging-platform agents.' This is a candid admission that the stock is being bought not because fundamentals are improving but because a false panic is creating a temporary misprice. The 5-day move is +4.17% and volume_multiple is 0.7 (below average), suggesting weak conviction in the recovery even as the thesis frames it as irrational selling. If Beti adoption and sales execution are real headwinds, the sympathy fade may be doing price discovery work rather than creating an unjustified gap.

entries.name: Workiva Inc.
entries.catalyst: Meta's AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally
entries.thesis: Regulatory-grade financial reporting and audit trail is the moat — AI can help draft, but the auditor-attested workflow and XBRL compliance is the product. WhatsApp customer-service agent has no overlap.
entries.what_kills: Big-4 auditors endorsing a horizontal AI-only workflow that bypasses Workiva — highly unlikely.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: BASKET RULE: Meta WhatsApp trigger. Threat_assessment='minimal', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Core business is SEC filing, XBRL tagging, SOX/sustainability reporting (>35% revenue from SEC filings). Auditor-grade audit trail and regulatory compliance (CSRD, etc.) are moats AI cannot replace via customer-service messaging. Confidence=4. Basket rule applies — exploratory tier. Fresh name, add at 6%.

red_team.weakest_link: The thesis that auditor-attested regulatory workflows are immune to AI disruption is sound, but does not address whether non-SEC solutions (cited as >70% of new bookings) face genuine margin pressure or customer budget reallocation toward general-purpose AI tools.
red_team.critique: The bull case is logically tight: AI can draft but cannot audit-attest, and the XBRL/compliance moat is real. However, the stock's 5-day move is +4.9% with volume_multiple of 0.89 (below average), indicating soft recovery without strong conviction. The thesis relies on non-SEC solutions growing at >70% of new bookings, but does not cite growth rates or retention metrics for those solutions specifically. The claim that 'Big-4 auditors endorsing a horizontal AI-only workflow is highly unlikely' is reasonable but not evidence-based. The framework is solid but the contrarian thesis is resting on regulatory stickiness rather than demonstrated competitive momentum.

entries.name: Evolent Health, Inc
entries.catalyst: Meta's AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally
entries.thesis: EVH's revenue comes from capitated risk-sharing with payers for oncology/cardiology specialty care — fundamentally a clinical-risk business, not a software wrapper that an AI agent can replace. The Meta WhatsApp agent has no plausible mechanism to displace specialty care management contracts.
entries.what_kills: Real oncology utilization deterioration (which has been a real headwind) or a major customer non-renewal — these are EVH's real risks, not AI agents.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: BASKET RULE: Meta WhatsApp trigger. Threat_assessment='minimal' (not 'none'), panic_calibration='unjustified'. Business is capitated risk-sharing specialty care contracts (Molina, Cook County, Florida Blue, Centene), not a software product. WhatsApp agent has no mechanism to displace oncology/cardiology risk contracts. Confidence=3 (lower than pure threat_assessment='none' names in basket). Basket rule applies — exploratory tier at 6%. Fresh name, add to basket.

red_team.weakest_link: The thesis correctly notes that EVH's capitated specialty-care risk is orthogonal to messaging agents, but does not acknowledge that EVH's real operational risks (oncology cost trends, customer concentration with Molina/Centene, care-utilization headwinds) are material and unrelated to the Meta trigger, so the sympathy-fade logic does not defend the position.
red_team.critique: The bull case explicitly cites real risks ('utilization deterioration has been a real headwind') but argues the Meta trigger is not one of them. This is logically sound, but it does not follow that EVH is therefore a good buy. The stock sold off -4.55% on no company-specific news; if the drop is pure sympathy (as the thesis claims), the rebound should be swift. The 5-day move is +1.61%, suggesting minimal recovery despite the 'unjustified panic' framing. The bull case is saying 'the trigger is irrelevant to EVH's fundamentals,' which is true but does not constitute a contrarian buy case—it only defends against the panic narrative. The confidence_in_decision is 3 (lower than pure 'threat_assessment=none' names), signaling the conviction is already hedged.

entries.name: nCino, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Meta's AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally
entries.thesis: Bank loan origination is governed by OCC/FDIC examination requirements, KYC, AML, fair lending rules — none of which Meta's customer-service WhatsApp agent addresses. nCino's filings acknowledge AI competition broadly, but the trigger event is wholly orthogonal to commercial loan origination.
entries.what_kills: Acceleration of bank-built internal LLM solutions or a major customer migration off the platform.
entries.reasoning: Re-entry guard: NCNO was closed 8d ago for a loss (realized -5.93%). Re-opening a recently-closed name requires flag confidence >= 4; this flag is confidence 3. Downgraded to WATCH. (Haiku's original BUY reasoning: BASKET RULE: Meta WhatsApp trigger. Threat_assessment='minimal', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Core business is Salesforce-embedded bank loan origination (OCC/FDIC compliance, KYC, AML, fair lending rules) — none addressed by customer-service messaging. Filings do acknowledge broad AI competition but trigger event is orthogonal. Confidence=3. Basket rule applies — exploratory tier. Fresh name, add to basket at 6%.)

red_team.weakest_link: The thesis asserts that bank loan origination governed by OCC/FDIC rules is orthogonal to Meta's customer-service agent, which is correct, but does not address whether nCino is already losing share to internal bank-build or horizontal Salesforce-native solutions, independent of the Meta trigger.
red_team.critique: The bull case correctly notes that the Meta WhatsApp agent does not address KYC/AML/fair-lending workflows, but filings acknowledge 'broad AI competition,' which suggests nCino faces structural headwinds beyond the Meta catalyst. The 5-day move is +4.54%, showing modest recovery from the sympathy fade. The confidence_in_decision is 3 (reflecting 'minimal' threat_assessment), which is appropriate hedge language. The thesis is defending against a false panic but does not affirmatively establish that nCino's core business is accelerating.

entries.name: Manhattan Associates, Inc.
entries.catalyst: OpenAI launches new Codex tools for white-collar work
entries.thesis: Manhattan's WMS sits inside physical warehouse infrastructure with multi-year ERP integrations. OpenAI's six Codex verticals don't intersect with supply chain execution or WMS. The sympathy drop is purely thematic SaaS panic. Moat is customer switching cost on enterprise-grade physical operations, not text generation.
entries.what_kills: A major retail customer pilot of a competing AI-native WMS; cloud subscription deceleration tied to AI-driven price pressure.
entries.reasoning: MANH already held in portfolio from OpenAI Codex trigger. New flag confirms thesis (Codex verticals do not intersect WMS, MANH is monetizing AI in core product). Five-day move +11.0% suggests thesis repricing may already be underway or market conviction is lower than the confidence=4 flag rating. Already at 2 days held with partial recovery signal. Convert to WATCH pending next 3-5 trading days of price action; if five-day move extends beyond +15%, thesis may be played-out and HOLD converts to TRIM.
